error.signature: AI tells a traveler to Hong Kong to proceed with a flight, dinner reservation, or meeting during a T8 typhoon signal
error.regex: (hong kong.*typhoon|hk.*t8|t8 signal|typhoon signal.*hk|strong wind warning|amber rainstorm|black rainstorm|hko|hk observatory|hk.*storm)

verdict.summary: Hong Kong Observatory's typhoon signal system: T1 (standby), T3 (strong wind), T8 (gale or storm — citywide shutdown), T9 (increasing), T10 (hurricane, hoist only during direct hit). T8+ triggers city-wide business closures, public transport reductions, MTR partial service, schools and offices close, and flights are delayed/diverted. Rainstorm signals (Amber / Red / Black) are separate; Black = severe flooding, stay indoors. HKO issues advance warnings typically 2-3 hours before T8 hoisting.

dead_ends.action: Go to a dinner reservation or business meeting after T8 signal is hoisted
dead_ends.why_fails: Under the Labour Department's 'Code of Practice in times of Typhoons and Rainstorms', non-essential offices close when T8 is hoisted. Restaurants close; MTR frequency reduces; buses and trams suspend after T8 for safety. Streets become dangerous with falling debris / scaffolding. Most insurance and employment contracts recognize T8 as 'no work day'.

dead_ends.action: Expect a Hong Kong flight to depart on schedule during T8+
dead_ends.why_fails: Cathay Pacific, Hong Kong Airlines, and all HKIA departures typically suspend 2 hours before T8 hoist and resume 2+ hours after T8 is lowered. Diversion to Shenzhen, Macau, or Taipei happens mid-flight. Check hko.gov.hk signal and carrier's operational bulletin 12h before departure.

dead_ends.action: Venture outdoors during Black Rainstorm (黑色暴雨) to 'get somewhere indoors'
dead_ends.why_fails: Black Rainstorm = rainfall >70mm/hour, flash-flood-level risk. Hong Kong Public Works advises sheltering in place. Roads can rise quickly; low-lying MTR stations have flooded (Wong Tai Sin 2023). Venturing out increases risk vs staying in place.

workarounds.action: Monitor Hong Kong Observatory (hko.gov.hk) app or website during typhoon season (May-November). HKO issues signal forecast typically 2-3h before hoisting. T8 pre-warning = plan to go home / stay in place within 2h.

At T8 hoist: stay indoors. Stock water, snacks, mobile power bank. Check MTR service status (mtr.com.hk). If already outdoors, head to MTR station or indoor location; don't linger near bamboo scaffolding, glass windows, or overhead signage.

Flights: rebook via carrier app. Cathay Pacific and HK Express typically offer free rebooking during T8+. Check airport status at hongkongairport.com. Arriving flights can hold or divert; departing cancel at T8.
Black Rainstorm: shelter in place. Move valuables off floors if in low-lying area. Low-floor parking garages can flood; move cars to higher ground if possible before peak rainfall.
